CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Mining and Mineral Resources Research Institute Act of 1984, and not as part of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 which comprises this chapter.

Amendments1988—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (a) of this section, those colleges or universities which, on , have a mining or mineral resources research institute program which has been found to be eligible pursuant to title III of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 () shall continue to be eligible pursuant to this subchapter for a period of four fiscal years beginning .”Pub. Amendments1988—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (a) of this section, those colleges or universities which, on , have a mining or mineral resources research institute program which has been found to be eligible pursuant to title III of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 () shall continue to be eligible pursuant to this subchapter for a period of four fiscal years beginning .”Pub. L. 100–48391 Stat. 445August 29, 1984October 1, 1984

children.text: the presence of a substantial program of graduate instruction and research in mining or mineral extraction or closely related fields which has a demonstrated history of achievement;
evidence of institutional commitment for the purposes of this subchapter;
evidence that such institution has or can obtain significant industrial cooperation in activities within the scope of this subchapter; and

the presence of an engineering program in mining or minerals extraction that is accredited by the Accreditation Board for Engineering and Technology, or evidence of equivalent institutional capability as determined by the Committee.

Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (a), those colleges or universities which, on , have a mining or mineral resources research institute program which has been found to be eligible pursuant to this subchapter shall continue to be eligible subject to review at least once during the period authorized by the Mining and Mineral Resources Research Institute Amendments of 1988, under the provisions of subsection (a). The results of such review shall be submitted by , pursuant to section 11(a)(2) of the Mining and Mineral Resources Research Institute Amendments of 1988.October 12, 1988January 15, 1992

Generic mineral technology centers established by the Secretary under this subchapter are to be composed of institutes eligible pursuant to subsection (a). Existing generic mineral technology centers shall continue to be eligible under this subchapter subject to at least one review prior to , pursuant to section 11(a)(3) of the Mining and Mineral Resources Research Institute Amendments of 1988.January 15, 1992
